grant_title: WinnCompanies Secures Funding For $65 Million Rehab of the Historic Pines of Perinton Affordable Housing Complex
demographic_requirements: No specific demographic requirements mentioned; project focuses on preserving affordability for low-income families, including children (over one-third of residents are children).
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include non-profit organizations, government agencies, private developers, and affordable housing developers. The project is focused on the rehabilitation of the Pines of Perinton in Perinton-Fairport, NY, to extend affordability for at least 40 years and modernize all 508 units as Low-Income Housing Tax Credit units. All units will be affordable for various income categories, with 26 units converted to ADA accessible housing.
